Nonconformity Description: The Remote Working Policy defines requirements for secure remote access (VPN use, secure configuration, user responsibilities), but the ISMS provides no evidence of centralised enforcement, continuous monitoring of remote-access activity, or validation of endpoint compliance. Defined control intent is not translated into an enforced, evidenced technical control.
Objective Evidence (Audit Basis): Documentation review of the Remote Working Policy. No supporting evidence of MFA enforcement, endpoint compliance checks, or remote-access log review was available within the ISMS documentation set.
Root Cause: The Remote Working Policy was authored as a governance / expectation document without a corresponding technical enforcement standard, an assigned operational owner, or a defined monitoring requirement to translate policy into enforced configuration and auditable evidence.
Correction (Immediate Containment): Mandate Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) across all remote-access channels immediately (Entra ID / Azure AD Conditional Access or equivalent identity provider).
Corrective Action (Eliminate Root Cause): Implement centralised remote-access enforcement: MDM/Intune for endpoint compliance and BeyondTrust or Zscaler for privileged remote access. Route remote-access logs into a SIEM (Microsoft Sentinel or Splunk) with monthly review. Establish quarterly endpoint security-posture validation and assign a named operational owner.
Verification / Closure Criteria: 100% of remote-access sessions authenticated via MFA; endpoint compliance checks active; remote-access logs reviewed monthly with evidence retained; SIEM integration confirmed.

Nonconformity Description: The Information Asset Owner (IAO) role is formally defined in the Information Asset Owners Handbook, but there is insufficient evidence that IAO responsibilities are consistently executed: limited evidence of asset-level risk assessments, unclear maintenance of asset registers, and no standardised IAO-to-governance reporting.
Objective Evidence (Audit Basis): Review of the Information Asset Owners Handbook. No asset-level risk assessments, populated asset registers, or IAO reporting outputs were evidenced in the documentation reviewed.
Root Cause: Asset ownership was defined organisationally but not operationalised procedurally. The ISMS lacks a defined reporting cadence, standardised templates, and a central validation function to make IAO accountability evidenced and enforceable.
Correction (Immediate Containment): Issue an interim standardised IAO reporting template and require IAOs to confirm current asset assignments and classifications.
Corrective Action (Eliminate Root Cause): Establish a quarterly IAO reporting cycle to the governance board; require periodic asset-level risk assessments (minimum annual, with mid-year exception reporting); implement centralised asset-inventory tracking with audit oversight (interim SharePoint register with version control acceptable); define and baseline asset-ownership KPIs.
Target Date: 09 Jul 2026 (template + KPI framework); 08 Aug 2026 (first governance submission)
Verification / Closure Criteria: 100% of in-scope assets assigned an active IAO with a dated risk assessment on file; quarterly IAO report tabled at the governance board; KPIs baselined and reported.

Nonconformity Description: A documented risk-management methodology aligned with ISO principles exists but is not operating as a live process. There is no evidence that risk assessments are performed on a scheduled basis, that the risk register is updated with new or changed risks, or that risk-treatment decisions are documented and tracked to completion.
Objective Evidence (Audit Basis): Review of the Risk Management Policy and Methodology. No evidence of a maintained central risk register, scheduled risk reviews, or tracked treatment plans was available.
Root Cause: The methodology was established at the policy layer but never assigned a process owner or embedded into a recurring governance cycle. The risk register exists as a static artefact with no trigger, cadence, or accountability driving its maintenance.
Correction (Immediate Containment): Designate a named Risk Register Owner and perform an immediate review / refresh of the central risk register.
Corrective Action (Eliminate Root Cause): Embed a quarterly risk-review cycle as a standing governance-board agenda item; require all IAOs to submit risk assessments against their asset portfolios; define an escalation process for risks rated High/Critical; integrate risk outputs into decision-making and control validation.
Target Date: 09 Jun 2026 (owner designated + register reviewed); 09 Jul 2026 (first governance submission)
Verification / Closure Criteria: Active risk register with dated entries; all High-rated risks have an assigned owner and treatment plan; risk register reviewed at the governance board with documented minutes.

Nonconformity Description: There is no ongoing visibility into whether implemented controls perform as intended. No evidence of audit-log reviews, no defined Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) or Key Control Indicators (KCIs), and no monitoring dashboard or reporting mechanism to detect control failure between formal audit cycles. Directly corresponds to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 control 8.16 (Monitoring activities).
Objective Evidence (Audit Basis): Documentation review. No KRI/KCI definitions, log-review records, or monitoring outputs were evidenced.
Root Cause: The ISMS defines controls but did not define how control performance is measured. No Clause 9.1 monitoring-and-measurement programme, KRIs/KCIs, or log-review responsibilities were specified, leaving no mechanism to detect control degradation.
Correction (Immediate Containment): Begin monthly review of currently available access and system logs and record the outcomes.
Corrective Action (Eliminate Root Cause): Define a KRI/KCI framework for the four highest-risk domains (remote access, asset management, risk management, incident response), each with thresholds, owners, and review frequency; implement centralised logging/monitoring with a monthly-reviewed dashboard; establish a quarterly control-performance review reported to the CISO/governance board; retain monitoring evidence.
Action Owner: Information Security Manager / IT Operations Lead
Target Date: 24 Jun 2026 (KRI/KCI framework); 08 Aug 2026 (first monthly monitoring report)
Verification / Closure Criteria: Documented KRI/KCI framework in place; monthly monitoring reports produced and retained for a minimum of 12 months; control performance tabled at the governance board quarterly.

Nonconformity Description: Not a nonconformity. The policy framework is comprehensive and well-aligned with ISO 27001, but opportunities exist to improve integration between policies and operational processes, enhance cross-domain visibility of control implementation, and strengthen linkages between governance, risk, and technical controls.
Objective Evidence (Audit Basis): Documentation review indicates strong individual policies developed largely as discrete documents rather than as an integrated, cross-referenced management system.
Root Cause: Policies were developed as standalone documents rather than as an integrated management system with cross-references and a unifying governance view. This reflects ISMS maturity stage rather than a control defect.
Correction (Immediate Containment): N/A — improvement opportunity (not a control failure).
Corrective Action (Eliminate Root Cause): Align policies with a centralised governance dashboard and reporting structure; build a risk–control–outcome traceability matrix to link findings to controls and remediation.
Verification / Closure Criteria: Governance dashboard operational; documented risk–control–outcome traceability matrix maintained.
